description_long: This is a stone-clad house with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in weathered shingles, featuring two prominent chimneys. The facade is adorned with green-trimmed windows, some of which are partially covered by climbing ivy, adding a touch of rustic charm. The main entryway is sheltered by a small porch with a tiled floor and a gabled roof, supported by stone columns. The house includes a side extension and a semi-circular turret-like structure, as well as a covered veranda with open stone balustrades. The windows are arranged in an asymmetrical pattern, and the overall structure suggests a traditional English cottage or Tudor Revival style. The exterior materials and architectural details evoke a sense of historical character and durability.
description: A stone house with a steep gable roof, green-trimmed windows, ivy accents, and a covered porch entryway. Features include a turret-like extension and a rustic, traditional design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main gabled facade, green windows, ivy, and the covered porch entryway.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the main entry, side extension, and the semi-circular turret structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back wall, a single small window, and the rear entrance under a gabled roof.
description_view_3: Side view showing the stone turret, covered veranda with balustrades, and the steep roof profile.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with gabled roof, stone columns, tiled floor, and decorative ivy.
